back_translation: When he (royal-register) departed from the temple precincts, one of his disciples said respectfully to him, 'Teacher, look how great these stones and buildings are!'
Jesus answered him, 'Do you see these great buildings? Not a single stone will be left resting on another here; every one will be torn down and brought crashing down.'
While he (royal-register) was seated on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple precincts, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
'Please tell us, when will these things happen, and what is the sign that all these things are about to be fulfilled?'
Jesus began to say to them (royal-register), 'Be on your guard that no one leads you astray.'
'Many will come claiming my name; they will say, "I am that one," and will lead many into error.'
'When you hear news of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed and panicked. These things must happen, but the end is not yet.'
'For nation will rise against nation, kingdom will rise against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places, there will be famines. These things are the beginning of the pains before birth.'

'But you yourselves, watch out carefully, for they will hand you over to councils, you will be beaten in the synagogues, and because of me you will be made to stand before governors and kings, as a witness to them.'
'And the good news must first be proclaimed to all nations.'
'When they take you away and hand you over, do not worry beforehand about what to say. But whatever is given to you at that time, speak that; for the one speaking is not you, but the Holy Spirit.'
'Brother-and-sibling will betray brother-and-sibling to death; a father will betray his own child; children will rise up against their father-and-mother and cause them to be killed.'
'And you will be hated by all people because of my name. But the one who endures to the end, that one will be saved.'

'When you see «the detestable thing that causes desolation» standing in the place where it should not be (may the reader understand), then let those who are in the region of Judea flee to the mountains.'
'Let the one on the flat rooftop not come down, and not go into the house to take anything out.'
'And let the one who is in the field not return to fetch his outer cloak.'
'In those days, how deeply to be pitied for women who are pregnant and mothers who are nursing a child.'
'Pray that this event will not happen in the cold/winter season.'
'For in those days there will be tribulation such as has never occurred like this from the beginning of the (royally-rendered) creation that God created until now, and will not occur again.'

'If the Lord (royally-rendered, the YHWH-bearing Lord) had not shortened those days, no human person would be saved. But because of the ones-he-has-chosen whom he chose-and-selected, he has shortened those days.'
'At that time, if anyone says to you, "Look, the Christ is here," or "Look, he is there," do not believe it.'
'For false-Christs and false-prophets will appear and will display signs and miraculous wonders to lead astray even the elect-ones, if that were possible.'
'But you, watch out carefully; I have told you everything in advance.'
'But in those days, after that tribulation, «the sun will grow dark, and the moon will not shine its light,»'
'«the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the heaven-skies will be shaken.»'
'At that time, people will see the Son of Man coming (royal-register) in the midst of clouds with great almighty-power and glory.'

'And at that time, he will send (royal-register) the angels of heaven to gather together his chosen-ones from the four winds/directions, from the farthest edge of the earth to the farthest edge of the heaven-sky.'
'Learn the parable from the fig tree: when its branches become tender and put out leaves, you know that the hot season is near.'
'In the same way, when you see these things happening, know that it-is-near, already at the door.'
'I tell you the truth: the people of this present generation will not pass away until all these things come to pass.'
'The heaven-sky and the earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away at all.'
'But about that day or that hour, no one knows. Even the angels of heaven do not know; even the (royally-rendered) Son does not know. Only the (royally-rendered) Father knows.'
'Be on your guard and stay awake watching, for you do not know when the appointed time will arrive.'
'It is like a man who traveled to a foreign land, leaving his house, and gave authority to each of his slaves to do his own work, then commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.'
'Therefore keep watch, for you do not know when the owner of the house will come — whether it is the evening, midnight, cockcrow, or early dawn.'
'Lest when he comes suddenly, he find you sleeping.'
'And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Keep watch!'
